title: Add constant folding for llvm.experimental.cttz.elts #182324
author: KavinTheG
description:
Resolves #182324. 

Modifies ConstantFoldIntrinsicCall2 to add constant folding for llvm.experimental.cttz.elts.

comments.author: KavinTheG
comments.body:
@lukel97, Gentle ping for review. 

Summarized changes below 
- Adds constant folding to llvm.experimental.cttz 
- Perhaps I misinterpreted https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/182324#issuecomment-3942364270, but I removed `-force-tail-folding-style=none` in pr58811-scev-expansion.ll to produce the llvm.experimental.cttz.elt intrinsic, and added the instcombine pass to constant fold it.

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM CODE FORMAT COMMENT: {undef deprecator}-->


:warning: undef deprecator found issues in your code. :warning:

<details>
<summary>
You can test this locally with the following command:
</summary>

``````````bash
git diff -U0 --pickaxe-regex -S '([^a-zA-Z0-9#_-]undef([^a-zA-Z0-9_-]|$)|UndefValue::get)' 'HEAD~1' HEAD llvm/test/Transforms/InstSimplify/ConstProp/cttz-elts.ll llvm/lib/Analysis/ConstantFolding.cpp
``````````

</details>


The following files introduce new uses of undef:
 - llvm/test/Transforms/InstSimplify/ConstProp/cttz-elts.ll

[Undef](https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#undefined-values) is now deprecated and should only be used in the rare cases where no replacement is possible. For example, a load of uninitialized memory yields `undef`. You should use `poison` values for placeholders instead.

In tests, avoid using `undef` and having tests that trigger undefined behavior. If you need an operand with some unimportant value, you can add a new argument to the function and use that instead.

For example, this is considered a bad practice:
```llvm
define void @fn() {
  ...
  br i1 undef, ...
}
```

Please use the following instead:
```llvm
define void @fn(i1 %cond) {
  ...
  br i1 %cond, ...
}
```

Please refer to the [Undefined Behavior Manual](https://llvm.org/docs/UndefinedBehavior.html) for more information.

comments.author: KavinTheG
comments.body:
- Rebased to have two commits, 1 for the precommit test
- Fixed the iszeropoison logic to return Poison only if all values are zeros & the Op2 is true.
- Added handling for undef and poison values 
- Added test case with recommended test case as well as 
  - a boolean vector (v4i1), it seems that boolean vectors are represented by `ConstantVector` while integers are represented by `ConstantDataVector`. This is why I used Operands[0] as it is instead  
  - poison and undef tests where the undef/poison lane is after a non-zero value and before.

comments.author: KavinTheG
comments.body:
Apologies about the rebase, and thanks for letting me know! Will keep that in mind. 

This commit addresses the undef/poison and treating it as 0 in this case. I think this matches the behaviour of llc. 
Also:
- removes changes done to loop vectorizer test. 
- changes pass used in cttz-elts.ll from instcombine to instsimplify to match other test cases in the same directory.

comments.author: KavinTheG
comments.body: This patch ensures `Poison` is returned if  the return type is not big enough for the number elements in the vector, as well as a test case to verify this behaviour.
